metadata.name: hyphe-traph
metadata.summary: A Trie/Graph hybrid memory structure used by the Hyphe crawler to index pages & webentities.

modules.name: traph.lru_trie.walk_history
documentation: Class tracking some information about a given walk in the LRU Trie.
Class representing the history of a simple walk into the Trie

modules.name: traph.lru_trie.header
documentation: Class representing the header of the LRU Trie buffer. This header can be
used to store various metadata and/or state.

methods.name: traph.lru_trie.header.LRUTrieHeader.read
documentation: Method used to set a switch to another block

methods.name: traph.lru_trie.header.LRUTrieHeader.pack
documentation: Method used to pack the node to binary form

methods.name: traph.lru_trie.header.LRUTrieHeader.write
documentation: Method used to write the node's data to storage

modules.name: traph.helpers
documentation: Miscellaneous helper functions used throughout the code.

functions.name: traph.helpers.https_variation
documentation: Returning the http(s) variation of the given lru

functions.name: traph.helpers.lru_variations
documentation: Returning the www/http(s) variations of the given lru

functions.name: traph.helpers.lru_iter
documentation: Returning an iterator over a lru's stems.

functions.name: traph.helpers.detailed_chunks_iter
documentation: Returning an iterator over a string's chunks of the given size.

modules.name: traph.link_store.link_store
documentation: Class representing the structure storing the links as linked list of stubs.
Note that linked lists are stored in reverse to allow for constant time
addition to the list and that link weights are stored as duplication.

methods.name: traph.link_store.link_store.LinkStore.link_nodes_iter
documentation: NOTE: this method will yield blocks as is, so expect duplication!

modules.name: traph.link_store.header
documentation: Class representing the header of the Link Store buffer. This header can be
used to store various metadata and/or state.

methods.name: traph.link_store.header.LinkStoreHeader.read
documentation: Method used to set a switch to another block

methods.name: traph.link_store.header.LinkStoreHeader.pack
documentation: Method used to pack the node to binary form

methods.name: traph.link_store.header.LinkStoreHeader.write
documentation: Method used to write the node's data to storage

modules.name: traph.storage.file
documentation: Class abstracting random access file handling for the Traph.

methods.name: traph.storage.file.FileStorage.count_blocks
documentation: Method returning the number of blocks

methods.name: traph.storage.file.FileStorage.check_for_corruption
documentation: Method returning whether the file is corrupted

methods.name: traph.storage.file.FileStorage.read
documentation: Method reading a block in the file and returning the contained node

modules.name: traph.lru_trie.node
documentation: Class representing a single node from the LRU trie.
Note that it should be possible to speed up targeted updates by only
writing the updated fields (typically when setting a pointer).
TODO: we can be more compact for some things

methods.name: traph.lru_trie.node.LRUTrieNode.read
documentation: set a switch to another block

methods.name: traph.lru_trie.node.LRUTrieNode.refresh
documentation: re-acquiring data from storage because it may have changed

methods.name: traph.lru_trie.node.LRUTrieNode.pack
documentation: pack the node to binary form

methods.name: traph.lru_trie.node.LRUTrieNode.write
documentation: write the node's data to storage

methods.name: traph.lru_trie.node.LRUTrieNode.is_root
documentation: Method returning whether this node is the root

methods.name: traph.lru_trie.node.LRUTrieNode.has_left
documentation: know whether the left block is set

methods.name: traph.lru_trie.node.LRUTrieNode.has_right
documentation: know whether the left block is set

methods.name: traph.lru_trie.node.LRUTrieNode.has_child
documentation: know whether the child block is set

methods.name: traph.lru_trie.node.LRUTrieNode.has_parent
documentation: know whether the parent block is set

methods.name: traph.lru_trie.node.LRUTrieNode.has_outlinks
documentation: know whether the outlinks block is set

methods.name: traph.lru_trie.node.LRUTrieNode.has_inlinks
documentation: know whether the outlinks block is set

methods.name: traph.lru_trie.node.LRUTrieNode.has_webentity
documentation: know whether the node has a webentity flag

methods.name: traph.lru_trie.node.LRUTrieNode.webentity
documentation: retrieve the webentity id of the flag

methods.name: traph.lru_trie.node.LRUTrieNode.unset_webentity
documentation: remove the tie between the node (ie. prefix) and the webentity

modules.name: traph.storage.memory
documentation: Class storing the data in a byte array.

methods.name: traph.storage.memory.MemoryStorage.count_blocks
documentation: Method returning the number of blocks

methods.name: traph.storage.memory.MemoryStorage.read
documentation: Method reading a block in the bytearray

methods.name: traph.storage.memory.MemoryStorage.write
documentation: Method writing nodes to the bytearray

modules.name: traph.storage.memmap
documentation: Class abstracting reading the given file using python's mmap module

documentation: Method reading a block in the map and returning the contained node

methods.name: traph.storage.memmap.MemMapStorage.release
documentation: Method releasing the map from memory

modules.name: traph.link_store.node
documentation: Class representing a single node from the Link Store.
Note: it could be useful to create an abstract Node class for both the
LinkStoreNode and the LRUTrieNode but I thinks this is overkill for the

methods.name: traph.link_store.node.LinkStoreNode.read
documentation: Method used to set a switch to another block

methods.name: traph.link_store.node.LinkStoreNode.pack
documentation: Method used to pack the node to binary form

methods.name: traph.link_store.node.LinkStoreNode.write
documentation: Method used to write the node's data to storage

methods.name: traph.link_store.node.LinkStoreNode.is_root
documentation: Method returning whether this node is the root

methods.name: traph.link_store.node.LinkStoreNode.has_previous
documentation: Method used to know whether the previous block is set

methods.name: traph.link_store.node.LinkStoreNode.previous
documentation: Method used to retrieve the previous block

methods.name: traph.link_store.node.LinkStoreNode.set_previous
documentation: Method used to set a sibling

documentation: Method used to read the previous sibling

documentation: Method used to get previous node

documentation: Method used to know whether the target block is set

documentation: Method used to retrieve the target block

methods.name: traph.link_store.node.LinkStoreNode.set_target
documentation: Method used to set the target block

modules.name: traph.traph_write_report
documentation: Class representing the report we will emit on write operations to be able
to return some stats & the created webentities to the user.

modules.name: traph.lru_trie.lru_trie
documentation: Class representing the Trie indexing the LRUs.

methods.name: traph.lru_trie.lru_trie.LRUTrie.__ensure_stem_from_siblings
documentation: Method ensuring that a sibling with the desired char exists

methods.name: traph.lru_trie.lru_trie.LRUTrie.add_lru
documentation: Method adding a lru to the trie

methods.name: traph.lru_trie.lru_trie.LRUTrie.add_page
documentation: Method adding a page to the trie

methods.name: traph.lru_trie.lru_trie.LRUTrie.dfs_iter
documentation:
Note that childless_paths refers to the upper webentity tree, not
the pages' one.

methods.name: traph.lru_trie.lru_trie.LRUTrie.webentity_dfs_iter
documentation:
Note that this algorithm will peruse the webentity nodes only for the
given prefix. We would need a refined algorithm for the cases when
then prefixes are not given and we need to peruse the webentity's
whole realm.

methods.name: traph.lru_trie.lru_trie.LRUTrie.webentity_inorder_iter
documentation:
Note that it would be fairly easy to resume traversal from any given
lru. It could simplify the pagination token scheme.

modules.name: traph.traph
documentation: Main class representing the Traph data structure.

methods.name: traph.traph.Traph.add_webentity_creation_rule_iter
documentation:
Note: write_in_trie has 2 effects: store the rule in the trie, and apply it to existing entities.
write_in_trie=False is essentially for init on an existing traph.

Note 2: it seems obvious from the api design but let's restate it:
We can only have one rule for each prefix (or none).

methods.name: traph.traph.Traph.create_webentity
documentation: Note: will raise an error if any of the prefixes is already defining an existing entity

methods.name: traph.traph.Traph.delete_webentity
documentation:
Note: weid is only useful to check data consistency, but not strictly necessary to the method.
It there is no weid, a consistency check will be skipped but the method will execute regardless.

methods.name: traph.traph.Traph.move_prefix_to_webentity
documentation:
Note: pay attention to the unintuitive order of arguments.
You may prefer the more explicit alias move_prefix_to_webentity_from_webentity

methods.name: traph.traph.Traph.move_prefix_to_webentity_from_webentity
documentation: A more explicit alias of move_prefix_to_webentity

methods.name: traph.traph.Traph.retrieve_prefix
documentation:
Returns the closest prefix to contain the LRU
(the prefix defining the webentity that the LRU belongs to)
Note: does not apply creation rules. For that feature see get_potential_prefix()

methods.name: traph.traph.Traph.get_potential_prefix
documentation:
Returns the longest of prefixes or "potential prefixes" ie. from creation rules.
Very similar to internal method __add_page except it does not add the lru.

methods.name: traph.traph.Traph.retrieve_webentity
documentation:
Returns the closest webentity id to contain the LRU
(the webentity it belongs to)

methods.name: traph.traph.Traph.get_webentity_pages_iter
documentation: Note: the prefixes are supposed to match the webentity id. We do not check.

methods.name: traph.traph.Traph.get_webentity_crawled_pages_iter
documentation: Note: the prefixes are supposed to match the webentity id. We do not check.

methods.name: traph.traph.Traph.get_webentity_most_linked_pages_iter
documentation:
Returns a list of objects {lru:, indegree:}
Note: the prefixes are supposed to match the webentity id. We do not check.

methods.name: traph.traph.Traph.get_webentity_parent_webentities
documentation: Note: the prefixes are supposed to match the webentity id. We do not check.

methods.name: traph.traph.Traph.get_webentity_child_webentities_iter
documentation: Note: the prefixes are supposed to match the webentity id. We do not check.

methods.name: traph.traph.Traph.get_webentity_pagelinks_iter
documentation:
Returns all or part of: pagelinks to the entity, internal pagelinks, pagelinks out of the entity.
Default is only internal pagelinks.
Note: the prefixes are supposed to match the webentity id. We do not check.

methods.name: traph.traph.Traph.get_webentity_outlinks_iter
documentation:
Returns the list of cited web entities
Note: the prefixes are supposed to match the webentity id. We do not check.

methods.name: traph.traph.Traph.get_webentity_outdegree
documentation:
Convenience method relying on get_webentity_outlinks (thus NOT more efficient)
Note: the prefixes are supposed to match the webentity id. We do not check.

methods.name: traph.traph.Traph.get_webentity_inlinks_iter
documentation:
Returns the list of citing web entities
Note: the prefixes are supposed to match the webentity id. We do not check.

methods.name: traph.traph.Traph.get_webentity_indegree
documentation:
Convenience method relying on get_webentity_inlinks (thus NOT more efficient)
Note: the prefixes are supposed to match the webentity id. We do not check.

methods.name: traph.traph.Traph.get_webentity_degree
documentation:
Note: relies on get_webentity_inlinks() and get_webentity_outlinks(),
thus not more efficient than calling these.

methods.name: traph.traph.Traph.get_page_indegree
documentation: Convenience method relying on get_page_links (thus NOT more efficient)

methods.name: traph.traph.Traph.get_page_outdegree
documentation: Convenience method relying on get_page_links (thus NOT more efficient)

methods.name: traph.traph.Traph.get_page_degree
documentation: Convenience method relying on get_page_links (thus NOT more efficient)

methods.name: traph.traph.Traph.get_webentities_links_slow_iter
documentation:
This method should be slower than the regular version but lighter in
memory as it does not retain a full map of pages association to
webentities but only the least recently used ones.

methods.name: traph.traph.Traph.get_webentities_links_iter
documentation:
This method should be faster than the slow version because it avoids
unnecessary upward traversal.

Note that it is also possible to solve the links right away and store
them to solve their webentities later but this is most costly in RAM.

methods.name: traph.traph.Traph.add_page
documentation: Returns a webentity creation report as {created_webentities: {weid:[prefixes], ...}}

methods.name: traph.traph.Traph.webentity_page_nodes_iter
documentation: Note: the prefixes are supposed to match the webentity id. We do not check.
